title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: vignette walks create_*() functions build PK-Sim Building blocks named arguments, add_*() / remove_*() mutators attach snapshot. use Midazolam template throughout:

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_snapshot() -scratch counterpart load_snapshot(). returns -memory Snapshot carries current supported PK-Sim version Building blocks, touches files, path. populate add_*() verbs serialize export_snapshot(). rest vignette mutates Midazolam-based snapshot object, empty snapshot uses empty object disturb narrative.
code: empty <- create_snapshot(name = "My Project", description = "Notes") empty <- add_compound(empty, create_compound(name = "Drug X")) empty #>  #> ── PKSIM Snapshot ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Version: 81 (PKSIM 13.0) #> • Compounds: 1

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_individual() builds Individual demographic arguments. PK-Sim seeds underlying physiological tree simulation time. demographic field takes value-object helper (age(), weight(), height(), gestational_age()) carries value unit; helper owns default unit validates supplied unit. Extra demographics (e.g. gestational_age) accepted way: Attach result snapshot add_individual(): Parameters sit specific path inside individual’s physiological tree built create_parameter() using path argument. PK-Sim writes path pipe-separated segments (Organism|Liver|...): can pass list parameters straight create_individual(parameters = ...), assign individual$parameters fact (see “Modifying existing building blocks” ).
code: patient <- create_individual(   name = "Patient_001",   species = "Human",   population = "European_ICRP_2002",   gender = "FEMALE",   age = age(35),   weight = weight(65),   height = height(165) )  patient #>  #> ── Individual: Patient_001 ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Characteristics ── #>  #> • Species: Human #> • Population: European_ICRP_2002 #> • Gender: FEMALE #> • Age: 35 year(s) #> • Height: 165 cm #> • Weight: 65 kg pregnant_patient <- create_individual(   name = "Pregnant_Patient",   species = "Human",   population = "European_ICRP_2002",   gender = "FEMALE",   age = age(28),   weight = weight(70),   height = height(168),   gestational_age = gestational_age(20) # weeks )  pregnant_patient #>  #> ── Individual: Pregnant_Patient ──────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Characteristics ── #>  #> • Species: Human #> • Population: European_ICRP_2002 #> • Gender: FEMALE #> • Age: 28 year(s) #> • Gestational Age: 20 week(s) #> • Height: 168 cm #> • Weight: 70 kg snapshot <- add_individual(snapshot, patient) snapshot$individuals #>  #> ── Individuals (3) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • European (P-gp modified, CYP3A4 36 h) #> • Korean (Yu 2004 study) #> • Patient_001 ehc <- create_parameter(   path = "Organism|Liver|EHC continuous fraction",   value = 0.4 )  gfr <- create_parameter(   path = "Organism|Kidney|GFR",   value = 120,   unit = "ml/min",   source = "Literature",   description = "Normal adult GFR",   source_id = 123 )  gfr #>  #> ── Parameter: Organism|Kidney|GFR  #> • Value: 120 #> • Unit: ml/min #> • Source: Literature #> • Description: Normal adult GFR

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_formulation() builds Formulation type parameters. type argument open rather closed set. Seven curated human aliases resolve known PK-Sim release profile unlock per-type curated parameter vocabulary: "Dissolved", "Weibull", "Lint80", "Particle", "Table", "Zero Order", "First Order". non-empty string accepted verbatim written FormulationType, can author new unknown PK-Sim template type way create_event() accepts arbitrary template. parameters argument accepts two mutually exclusive forms. curated alias form takes bare scalar vector values keyed per-type alias vocabulary, exists seven known types. example, "Dissolved" takes none, "Weibull" accepts dissolution_time, dissolution_shape, lag_time, related keys. See ?create_formulation full parameter list per type. raw form takes list create_parameter() objects (raw list(Name =, Value =, ...) dicts) written straight formulation’s Parameters, preserving Unit, ValueOrigin, TableFormula verbatim, type. raw form valid form unknown type. raw form lets set arbitrary parameter Name, per-parameter ValueOrigin (source description), custom TableFormula type, including "Dissolved" unknown types:
code: oral_solution <- create_formulation(   name = "Oral Solution 10mg/mL",   type = "Dissolved" )  oral_solution #>  #> ── Formulation: Oral Solution 10mg/mL ────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Type: Dissolved  tablet <- create_formulation(   name = "Immediate Release Tablet",   type = "Weibull",   parameters = list(     dissolution_time = 30,     dissolution_time_unit = "min",     lag_time = 5,     lag_time_unit = "min",     dissolution_shape = 1.2,     suspension = TRUE   ) )  tablet #>  #> ── Formulation: Immediate Release Tablet ─────────────────────────────────────── #> • Type: Weibull #>  #> ── Parameters ── #>  #> • Dissolution time (50% dissolved): 30 min #> • Lag time: 5 min #> • Dissolution shape: 1.2 #> • Use as suspension: 1 raw_dissolved <- create_formulation(   name = "Suspension",   type = "Dissolved",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(       name = "Use as suspension",       value = 1,       source = "Lit",       description = "Reference XYZ"     )   ) )  raw_dissolved #>  #> ── Formulation: Suspension ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Type: Dissolved #>  #> ── Parameters ── #>  #> • Use as suspension: 1 snapshot <- add_formulation(snapshot, oral_solution) snapshot <- add_formulation(snapshot, tablet)

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_compound() builds compound validation common fields molecular_weight_unit. Compound-level parameters (clearances, halogens, …) passed list create_parameter() results: parameters named compound identified name, omit path argument.
code: drug <- create_compound(name = "Drug X")  drug <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   is_small_molecule = TRUE,   molecular_weight = 250.3,   plasma_protein_binding_partner = "Albumin",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(name = "Cl_spec", value = 5, unit = "ml/min/kg")   ) )  drug #>  #> ── Compound: Drug X ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Basic Properties ── #>  #> • Type: Small Molecule #> • Plasma Protein Binding Partner: Albumin #> • Molecular Weight: 250.3 g/mol #>  #> ── Physicochemical Properties ── #>  #> ── Additional Parameters ── #>  #> • Additional Parameters (1 total): #>   • Cl_spec: 5 ml/min/kg [Unknown]

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_process() builds compound process (plasma protein binding, metabolism, clearance, …) named arguments. internal_name identifies process template PK-Sim’s compound process repository (example "SpecificBinding", "MetabolizationSpecific_MM", "GlomerularFiltration"); data_source labels data origin. See ?create_process usage current examples; canonical list internal_name values maintained PK-Sim groups categories surfaced process$category (protein_binding_partners, metabolizing_enzymes, hepatic_clearance, transporter_proteins, renal_clearance, biliary_clearance, inhibition, induction). $category active binding derives domain label ("protein_binding_partners", "metabolizing_enzymes", "hepatic_clearance", "transporter_proteins", "renal_clearance", "biliary_clearance", "inhibition", "induction") internal_name. Compound processes loaded snapshot exposed flat named list Process objects via compound$processes, long-form processes tibble returned get_compounds_dfs() surfaces every (process, parameter) pair row.
code: binding <- create_process(   internal_name = "SpecificBinding",   data_source = "Buhr 1997",   molecule = "GABRG2" )  metabolism <- create_process(   internal_name = "MetabolizationSpecific_MM",   data_source = "Optimized",   molecule = "CYP3A4",   metabolite = "Hydroxy-Drug",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(name = "Km", value = 1.2, unit = "µmol/l"),     create_parameter(name = "kcat", value = 9.865, unit = "1/min")   ) )  metabolism$category #> [1] "metabolizing_enzymes"

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_population() builds Population recipe: settings used PK-Sim sample cohort simulation time. Use range() age, weight, height, BMI bounds.
code: adults <- create_population(   name = "Healthy Adults",   number_of_individuals = 50,   proportion_of_females = 50,   species = "Human",   source_population = "European_ICRP_2002",   age_range = range(20, 60, "year(s)"),   weight_range = range(50, 90, "kg") )  adults #>  #> ── Population: Healthy Adults ── #>  #> Source Population: European_ICRP_2002 #> Number of individuals: 50 #> Proportion of females: 50% #> Age range: 20 - 60 year(s) #> Weight range: 50 - 90 kg

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_expression_profile() builds ExpressionProfile. identity profile composite Molecule|Species|Category, three required, along molecule type.
code: cyp3a4 <- create_expression_profile(   molecule = "CYP3A4",   species = "Human",   category = "Healthy",   type = "Enzyme",   ontogeny = "CYP3A4" )  cyp3a4 #>  #> ── Expression Profile: CYP3A4 (Enzyme) ───────────────────────────────────────── #> • Species: Human #> • Category: Healthy #> • Ontogeny: CYP3A4

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_protocol() builds Protocol. default creates Simple Protocol (one application type, one dose, optional dosing interval); pass schemas create Advanced Protocol.
code: single_dose <- create_protocol(   name = "Single dose 10mg",   application_type = "Oral",   dosing_interval = "Single",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(name = "Start time", value = 0, unit = "h"),     create_parameter(name = "InputDose", value = 10, unit = "mg")   ) )  single_dose #>  #> ── Protocol: Single dose 10mg ────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Type: Simple #> • Application Type: Oral #> • Dosing Interval: Once #>  #> ── Parameters ── #>  #> • Start time: 0 h #> • InputDose: 10 mg

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: dosing schedules fit single application (multi-day course, two compounds dosed staggered schedules), use Advanced Protocol. Advanced Protocol built one Schemas; schema describes repeating block applications (example “10 mg orally every 24 hours 5 days”). Inside schema, individual application SchemaItem. build create_schema() (repeating block) create_schema_item() (one application), pass list schemas create_protocol(): application_type schema item validated canonical PK-Sim application types ("Oral", "Intravenous", "IntravenousBolus", "UserDefined").
code: qd_oral <- create_protocol(   name = "Once daily oral, 5 days",   schemas = list(     create_schema(       name = "Schema 1",       parameters = list(         create_parameter(name = "NumberOfRepetitions", value = 5),         create_parameter(name = "TimeBetweenRepetitions", value = 24, unit = "h")       ),       items = list(         create_schema_item(           name = "Item 1",           application_type = "Oral",           parameters = list(             create_parameter(name = "Start time", value = 0, unit = "h"),             create_parameter(name = "InputDose", value = 10, unit = "mg")           )         )       )     )   ),   time_unit = "h" )  qd_oral #>  #> ── Protocol: Once daily oral, 5 days ─────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Type: Advanced (Schema-based) #> • Time Unit: h #>  #> ── Schemas ── #>  #> • Schema: Schema 1 #>   • Item 1: Oral -

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: Event discrete perturbation drug administration: meal, change posture, enzyme inducer dose, exercise bout. create_event() builds one template (name event recipe PK-Sim’s event database) plus optional parameter overrides. PK-Sim clones named template simulation time applies overrides. set available templates defined inside PK-Sim (particular PK-Sim Events database ships installation), exact list valid template names depends PK-Sim setup. Templates commonly seen published snapshots include “Meal: Standard (Human)” family food effects; need template installation, define PK-Sim first re-export.
code: breakfast <- create_event(   name = "Breakfast",   template = "Meal: Standard (Human)",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(name = "Meal energy content", value = 500, unit = "kcal"),     create_parameter(name = "Meal volume", value = 0.3, unit = "l")   ) )  breakfast #>  #> ── Event: Breakfast ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Template: Meal: Standard (Human) #>  #> ── Parameters: ── #>  #> • Meal energy content: 500 kcal #> • Meal volume: 0.3 l

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: Observer simulation-time formula exposes derived quantity (example tissue concentration) natural model output. Observers travel groups called Observer sets. create_observer_set() builds set name list observers. Build observer create_observer(). validates input returns Observer typed accessors $name, $type, $dimension, $formula (full ExplicitFormula list), $formula_expression, $formula_dimension, $formula_references, $container_tags. companions create_formula_reference(), create_descriptor_condition(), create_molecule_list() build observer’s sub-properties. Bundle observers set attach set snapshot:
code: brain_obs <- create_observer(   name = "brain_plasma_conc",   type = "Container",   dimension = "Concentration (molar)",   formula = "Conc_Br",   formula_references = list(     create_formula_reference(       "Conc_Br",       "Organism|Brain|Plasma|Midazolam|Concentration"     )   ),   container_criteria = list(     create_descriptor_condition("Brain", "InContainer")   ),   molecule_list = create_molecule_list(for_all = FALSE, include = "Midazolam") )  brain_set <- create_observer_set(   name = "BrainPlasmaConcentration",   observers = list(brain_obs) )  brain_set #>  #> ── ObserverSet: BrainPlasmaConcentration ─────────────────────────────────────── #> • 1 observer  snapshot <- add_observer_set(snapshot, brain_set)

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_observed_data() builds ospsuite::DataSet value-object helpers time grid (time()), measurement values (values()), optional error series (error()). values dimension required supplied values(); gates unit validation. time() defaults unit "h", error, molecular_weight, lloq, metadata optional. create_observed_data() thin factory around loadDataSetFromSnapshot(), lower-level function converts raw snapshot observed-data structure ospsuite::DataSet. Call loadDataSetFromSnapshot() directly already hold raw structure (example slice read snapshot JSON) rather named-argument inputs. bridge also exposes two time helpers. convert_ospsuite_time_unit_to_lubridate() maps ospsuite time unit lubridate unit, convert_ospsuite_time_to_duration() maps value unit lubridate duration. "ks" (kilosecond) unit special case: duration helper converts seconds multiplying value 1000. observed-data export path asymmetric. loaded observed-data entry replays byte--byte original JSON export, post-load mutations loaded DataSet reflected export. DataSet added runtime add_observed_data() serialized lossy adapter round-trips osp.snapshots , PK-Sim round-trip unvalidated. Treat runtime-added observed data convenience R side, guaranteed PK-Sim round-trip.
code: obs <- create_observed_data(   name = "Phase I - Subject 001",   time = time(c(0, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 12, 24), unit = "h"),   values = values(     c(0, 12.5, 18.2, 15.8, 11.2, 6.8, 3.4, 1.1),     unit = "mg/l",     dimension = "Concentration (mass)"   ) )  obs #> <DataSet> #>   • Name: Phase I - Subject 001 #>   • X dimension: Time #>   • X unit: h #>   • Y dimension: Concentration (mass) #>   • Y unit: mg/l #>   • Error type: NULL #>   • Error unit: NULL #>   • Molecular weight: NULL #>   • LLOQ: NULL #> Meta data: convert_ospsuite_time_unit_to_lubridate("day(s)") #> [1] "days" convert_ospsuite_time_to_duration(2, "ks") #> [1] "2000s (~33.33 minutes)"

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: building-block kind paired add_*() remove_*() mutators take snapshot first return invisibly, chain base pipe. can pass several names remove :
code: snapshot <- remove_individual(snapshot, "Patient_001") snapshot <- remove_formulation(snapshot, "Oral Solution 10mg/mL") snapshot <- remove_individual(snapshot, c("Patient_001", "Patient_002")) snapshot <- remove_expression_profile(snapshot, "CYP3A4|Human|Healthy")

what: Adding several building blocks at once
title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: add_*() accepts either single building block list , can attach many one call. add_*() reports many entries added.
code: patients <- list(   create_individual("Patient_A", age = age(25), gender = "MALE"),   create_individual("Patient_B", age = age(45), gender = "FEMALE"),   create_individual("Patient_C", age = age(65), gender = "MALE") )  snapshot <- add_individual(snapshot, patients)  snapshot$individuals #>  #> ── Individuals (5) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • European (P-gp modified, CYP3A4 36 h) #> • Korean (Yu 2004 study) #> • Patient_A #> • Patient_B #> • Patient_C

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: fields existing building block mutable; assign like R6 active binding. Reading object shows effect: pattern works nested objects. bump parameter individual, look path individual$parameters write value: every change, parent snapshot exports updated values: export_snapshot(snapshot, ...) writes straight back JSON, extra step required.
code: patient <- snapshot$individuals$Patient_A patient #>  #> ── Individual: Patient_A ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Characteristics ── #>  #> • Gender: MALE #> • Age: 25 year(s) patient$age <- age(30) patient$weight <- weight(75) patient$gender <- "FEMALE" patient #>  #> ── Individual: Patient_A ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Characteristics ── #>  #> • Gender: FEMALE #> • Age: 30 year(s) #> • Weight: 75 kg korean <- snapshot$individuals$`Korean (Yu 2004 study)` ehc <- korean$parameters$`Organism|Liver|EHC continuous fraction` ehc #>  #> ── Parameter: Organism|Liver|EHC continuous fraction  #> • Value: 1 #> • Source: Unknown ehc$value <- 0.4 ehc #>  #> ── Parameter: Organism|Liver|EHC continuous fraction  #> • Value: 0.4 #> • Source: Unknown

title: Creating and managing building blocks
text: create_*() functions validate common inputs canonical ospsuite enums abort informative messages bad input: checks exported standalone helpers need outside create_*() call. returns TRUE success aborts informative message otherwise: validate_species() checks species ospsuite::Species. validate_gender() checks gender ospsuite::Gender. validate_population() checks population ospsuite::HumanPopulation, human population enum, general population list. validate_unit() checks unit dimension via ospsuite. validate_snapshot() checks object Snapshot.
code: tryCatch(   {     create_individual(       name = "Invalid Patient",       species = "InvalidSpecies",       gender = "OTHER"     )   },   error = function(e) cat("Error:", e$message, "\n") ) #> Error: Invalid species: InvalidSpecies  tryCatch(   {     create_formulation(       name = "Invalid Form",       type = "NonexistentType"     )   },   error = function(e) cat("Error:", e$message, "\n") ) #>  #> ── Formulation: Invalid Form ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Type: NonexistentType validate_species("Human") #> [1] TRUE validate_unit("mg/l", "Concentration (mass)") #> [1] TRUE

text: vignette("exporting-snapshots--dataframes") covers tibble layer analysis. full API reference https://esqlabs.github.io/osp.snapshots/reference/.

what: Reporting the inputs that drive a simulation
title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: PK-Sim snapshot mostly configuration: compounds, individuals, formulations, protocols, building blocks define simulation set . Observed data simulation results already easy report wider R ecosystem; awkward reporting inputs, live deep inside nested JSON tree. as_tibbles() flattens building blocks tidy tibbles, one concept per table, can filter dplyr render publication-quality tables. vignette pairs as_tibbles() gt turn configuration model report-ready HTML tables.

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles() takes snapshot plus optional kind naming collection(s) convert, returns either tibble named list tibbles collections split related tables. kind optional: omit (pass NULL) convert every kind , returned named list keyed kind; pass single kind get kind’s tibble list tibbles directly; pass vector kinds get named list keyed just requested kinds. Valid values kind: "compounds" "events" "expression_profiles" "formulations" "individuals" "observed_data" "observer_sets" "populations" "protocols" tibble layer read-: mutating returned tibble feed changes back snapshot. sections feature four kinds chosen different shape: single flat table (protocols), header split long-form parameter table (compounds, formulations), several related tables keyed id (individuals). Every kind follows one shapes, reporting recipe applies .

what: Compounds: a flat table and a long-form table
title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "compounds") returns list two tibbles: $properties carries one row per (compound, parameter) pair across Compound physicochemical properties. $processes long-form view: one row per (compound, process, parameter) triple across every biological Process (metabolism, transport, clearance, binding, …). physicochemical properties render directly report table:

previous_headings: Compounds: a flat table and a long-form table
title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: $processes tibble carries category column mirrors process$category R6 side. Filtering category dplyr::filter() gives view per-category groups compound, filtered result flows straight gt:
code: compound_dfs$processes |>   filter(category == "metabolizing_enzymes") |>   select(process_name, molecule, parameter, value, unit) |>   gt() |>   tab_header(title = "Metabolizing enzymes")

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "individuals") returns three tibbles joinable individual_id: $individuals one row per Individual demographic fields (species, population, gender, age, weight, height, gestational age, disease state, calculation methods). $individuals_parameters long-form view individual’s parameter tree: one row per (individual, parameter path) pair, value, unit, source, value-origin metadata. $individuals_expressions lists expression profiles attached individual: one row per (individual, profile) pair. focused selection demographic columns makes compact subject table:
code: individual_dfs <- as_tibbles(snapshot, "individuals") names(individual_dfs) #> [1] "individuals"             "individuals_parameters"  #> [3] "individuals_expressions"  individual_dfs$individuals |>   select(name, species, population, gender, age, age_unit, weight, weight_unit) |>   gt() |>   tab_header(title = "Individuals")

what: Formulations: a header split from its parameters
title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "formulations") returns two tibbles joinable formulation_id: $formulations one row per Formulation name release type. $formulations_parameters long-form view type-specific release parameters: one row per (formulation, parameter) pair, value unit.
code: formulation_dfs <- as_tibbles(snapshot, "formulations") names(formulation_dfs) #> [1] "formulations"            "formulations_parameters"  formulation_dfs$formulations |>   gt() |>   tab_header(title = "Formulations") formulation_dfs$formulations_parameters |>   select(name, value, unit) |>   gt() |>   tab_header(title = "Formulation release parameters")

title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: as_tibbles(snapshot, "protocols") returns single tibble whether snapshot protocols . Advanced Protocols contribute one row per Schema item, protocol-level, schema-level, item-level parameters joined name. simplest shape: one kind, one table, ready render.
code: as_tibbles(snapshot, "protocols") |>   select(protocol_name, dose, dose_unit, start_time, start_time_unit, dosing_interval) |>   gt() |>   tab_header(title = "Dosing protocols")

what: Every building block exports the same way
title: Exporting snapshots to data frames
text: four kinds cover every shape as_tibbles() produces. call works "events", "expression_profiles", "populations", "observer_sets": returns either single flat tibble header split long-form parameter table, ready filter() + gt() recipe. Kinds snapshot define come back empty (0-row) tibbles columns, reporting code never special-case missing building block. Passing kind converts everything , keyed kind name, handy assembling multi-table report: "observed_data" also available flattens every observed-data series long-form tibble, though measurement data rather configuration input.

title: Getting started with osp.snapshots
text: osp.snapshots provides R interface working PK-Sim project snapshots. package lets : Import PK-Sim snapshots JSON files, URLs, predefined templates. Navigate explore nested snapshot structure R6 objects. Modify building blocks parameters place. Export modified snapshots back JSON use PK-Sim. Convert building-block collections tibbles analysis R.

what: From a file, a URL, or a template
title: Getting started with osp.snapshots
text: load_snapshot() dispatches input. local .json path read disk; https?:// URL fetched; bare name resolved OSPSuite.BuildingBlockTemplates repository. guide use Midazolam template: load_snapshot() accepts v11.2 PK-Sim snapshots newer (Version >= 79); older snapshots use pre-v11 conventions package model. Re-export project PK-Sim loading needed.
code: # Local file snapshot <- load_snapshot("path/to/your_snapshot.json")  # Remote URL snapshot <- load_snapshot("https://example.com/snapshot.json")  # Named template (browse the catalogue with `osp_models()`) snapshot <- load_snapshot("Midazolam") snapshot <- load_snapshot("Midazolam") snapshot #>  #> ── PKSIM Snapshot ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Version: 80 (PKSIM 12.0) #> • Compounds: 1 #> • Events: 1 #> • ExpressionProfiles: 8 #> • Formulations: 2 #> • Individuals: 2 #> • ObservedData: 115 #> • ObservedDataClassifications: 47 #> • ParameterIdentifications: 3 #> • Protocols: 33 #> • SimulationClassifications: 4 #> • Simulations: 37

title: Getting started with osp.snapshots
text: loaded snapshot navigable tree: kind building block (individuals, compounds, formulations, populations, events, protocols, expression profiles, observer sets) lives named list snapshot$<kind> can list, look name, inspect interactively. Printing object prints readable summary; tab-completion works whole way .

title: Getting started with osp.snapshots
text: snapshot$individuals named list. title: Building simulations from scratch
text: whole workflow one call. Pass snapshot, name, exactly one individual population, configure compound inline protocol formulation use. Everything else optional. add_simulation() reported defaults derived snapshot (, Midazolam’s calculation methods alternatives) attached simulation. name resolved known building block, also get one informational warning per simulation; add proceeds either way. rest vignette explains arguments just used, optional inputs .

title: Building simulations from scratch
text: Compound building block (kind find snapshot$compounds) project-level definition drug: physicochemical properties, full menu available alternatives, process templates ships, . compound can referenced many simulations. compound configuration per-simulation overlay picks one path menu: calculation_methods override compound’s defaults, alternatives (e.g. solubility, permeability) selected, processes enabled, wired molecules systemic types, protocol used formulation fills application slot. snapshot entry point, add_simulation() derives referenced building block leave . point compound name resolver fills rest: calculation_methods: derived calculation methods defined referenced Compound building block. alternatives: alternative group defaulted group’s default alternative (one marked IsDefault, "User defined" fallback). Select specific alternative giving alternatives named vector, friendly property name alternative label, groups care ; every group name still defaulted . formulation (together protocol): single string binds protocol’s inferred first application slot, falling back literal "Formulation" key simple protocols. protocol one application slot, supply formulation named character vector mapping slot key formulation name, binding every slot explicitly. processes: never defaulted. Omitting processes produces process array, deselects compound’s processes PK-Sim (materialises right placeholder simulation runs explicitly disabled ). List processes want keep. name classified systemic-process type one "Hepatic", "Renal", "Biliary"; every name treated molecule name. protocol: omitted, compound included dosing protocol attached, administered simulation (useful compounds produced metabolically). required field name, must match compound snapshot.

title: Building simulations from scratch
text: Midazolam carries several solubility alternatives; default derivation pick one marked IsDefault, alternatives lets pick different one label: named group (solubility) overridden; every group (lipophilicity, fraction unbound, …) still defaulted compound. Selecting unknown property name label compound carry errors immediately, naming compound, property, available labels. protocol one application slot, bind every slot one call: create_protocol_selection() create_formulation_selection() remain available building Simulation object hand passing simulation argument; part inline compounds configuration .
code: names(snapshot$compounds[["Midazolam"]]$solubility) #> NULL  snapshot <- snapshot |>   add_simulation(     name = "Midazolam oral dose (FaSSIF solubility)",     individual = "Korean (Yu 2004 study)",     compounds = list(       list(         name = "Midazolam",         protocol = "po 15 mg",         formulation = "Oral solution",         processes = c("Hepatic"),         alternatives = c(solubility = "FaSSIF")       )     )   ) snapshot <- snapshot |>   add_simulation(     name = "Midazolam (multi-slot protocol)",     individual = "Korean (Yu 2004 study)",     compounds = list(       list(         name = "Midazolam",         protocol = "A protocol with two application slots",         formulation = c(Formulation = "Oral solution", "Formulation 2" = "IV solution")       )     )   )

title: Building simulations from scratch
text: simulation used compounds one event. remaining arguments optional; supply way, extra named arguments add_simulation() call.

title: Building simulations from scratch
text: Event building block (snapshot$events) project-level event definition. create_event_selection() picks one name supplies start_time (hours) simulation; aside start_time, events carry per-simulation overlay. ObserverSet building block (snapshot$observer_sets) project-level observer bundle. create_observer_set_selection() picks one name, per-simulation overlay , factory takes one argument. Midazolam template ships observer sets, template call look like:
code: event <- create_event_selection(name = "High-fat breakfast", start_time = 12) observer_set <- create_observer_set_selection(name = "BrainPlasmaConcentration")

title: Building simulations from scratch
text: create_solver_settings() produces sparse solver configuration; every argument optional. field omit filled PK-Sim’s built-CVODE defaults load, calling arguments supplying solver .

title: Building simulations from scratch
text: create_output_schema() collects one output intervals. output schema intervals means simulation reports time points, supply least one interval want output. create_output_interval() requires start_time, end_time, resolution (PK-Sim’s default units h, h, pts/h). Use output_selections (character vector full output-quantity paths) control quantities simulation reports.
code: schema <- create_output_schema(   intervals = list(     create_output_interval(start_time = 0, end_time = 2, resolution = 20),     create_output_interval(start_time = 2, end_time = 24, resolution = 20)   ) )

title: Building simulations from scratch
text: Simulation parameter overrides localized parameters, identified full container path inside simulation’s parameter tree. Use create_parameter(path = ...) obtain one.
code: overrides <- list(   create_parameter(     path = "Organism|Liver|Volume",     value = 1.8,     unit = "L"   ) )

title: Building simulations from scratch
code: snapshot <- snapshot |>   add_simulation(     name = "Midazolam oral dose (full)",     individual = "Korean (Yu 2004 study)",     compounds = list(       list(name = "Midazolam", protocol = "po 15 mg", formulation = "Oral solution", processes = c("Hepatic"))     ),     events = list(event),     solver = solver,     output_schema = schema,     output_selections = c(       "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Midazolam|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)"     ),     parameters = overrides   )

title: Create a new compound — create_compound
code: create_compound(   name,   description = NULL,   is_small_molecule = NULL,   plasma_protein_binding_partner = NULL,   molecular_weight = NULL,   molecular_weight_unit = "g/mol",   calculation_methods = NULL,   parameters = NULL,   lipophilicity = NULL,   fraction_unbound = NULL,   solubility = NULL,   intestinal_permeability = NULL,   permeability = NULL,   pKa = NULL,   processes = NULL )

title: Create a new compound — create_compound
text: name Character. Name compound (required). description Character. Free-text description compound. is_small_molecule Logical. Whether compound small molecule. Defaults TRUE PK-Sim omitted. plasma_protein_binding_partner Character. Name plasma protein binding partner: one "Unknown", "Albumin", "Glycoprotein". molecular_weight Numeric. Molecular weight value. molecular_weight_unit Character. Unit molecular weight. Defaults "g/mol". calculation_methods Character vector. Calculation method names PK-Sim uses derive compound quantities. parameters List Parameter objects (created create_parameter()) raw parameter lists attach additional compound parameters. set physicochemical properties; use dedicated arguments . lipophilicity lipophilicity() object, list objects define several named alternatives (mark exactly one default = TRUE designate group default; none marked, first element default), NULL. supplied, one Lipophilicity alternative created per element. fraction_unbound fraction_unbound() object, list objects define several named alternatives (mark exactly one default = TRUE designate group default; none marked, first element default), NULL. supplied, one FractionUnbound alternative created per element. solubility solubility() object, list objects define several named alternatives (mark exactly one default = TRUE designate group default; none marked, first element default), NULL. object expresses either scalar form (value reference pH, optional gain per charge) table form (pH/value table); list may mix forms. supplied, one Solubility alternative created per element. See solubility() scalar vs table forms mutual-exclusivity rule. intestinal_permeability intestinal_permeability() object, list objects define several named alternatives (mark exactly one default = TRUE designate group default; none marked, first element default), NULL. supplied, one IntestinalPermeability alternative created per element. permeability permeability() object, list objects define several named alternatives (mark exactly one default = TRUE designate group default; none marked, first element default), NULL. supplied, one Permeability alternative created per element. pKa List typed pKa entries, list type (one "Acid", "Base", "Neutral") numeric value, example list(list(type = "Base", value = 10.02)). Order preserved. NULL (default) empty list() leave created compound pKa types set. (clearing semantics, empty list() removes existing values, apply writable Compound$pka_types field, construction.) processes List Process objects (created create_process()) raw process lists attach compound.

title: Create a new compound — create_compound
Create a new compound — create_compound
code: # Create a minimal compound compound <- create_compound(name = "Drug X")  # Create a small molecule with molecular weight and binding partner compound <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   is_small_molecule = TRUE,   molecular_weight = 250.3,   plasma_protein_binding_partner = "Albumin" )  # Set the single-parameter physicochemical properties compound <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   lipophilicity = lipophilicity(2.5),   fraction_unbound = fraction_unbound(0.1),   intestinal_permeability = intestinal_permeability(1.14e-05),   permeability = permeability(0.0069) )  # Solubility with reference pH and gain per charge compound <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   solubility = solubility(9999, reference_pH = 7, gain_per_charge = 1000) )  # Table-based solubility (first column pH, second column value) compound <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   solubility = solubility(     table = data.frame(       pH = c(3, 6, 6.8),       value = c(5000, 3000, 90)     )   ) )  # Several named solubility alternatives (the first is the default) compound <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   solubility = list(     solubility(9999, name = "Aqueous"),     solubility(200, name = "FaSSIF")   ) )  # Several named solubility alternatives, explicitly marking the default compound <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   solubility = list(     solubility(9999, name = "Aqueous"),     solubility(200, name = "FaSSIF", default = TRUE)   ) )  # Multiple pKa entries compound <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   pKa = list(     list(type = "Base", value = 10.02),     list(type = "Acid", value = 1.7)   ) )  # Attach a process compound <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   processes = list(     create_process(       internal_name = "GlomerularFiltration",       data_source = "Publication X"     )   ) )  # Additional/loose parameters (not physicochemical properties) compound <- create_compound(   name = "Drug X",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(name = "Cl_spec", value = 5, unit = "ml/min/kg")   ) )

text: Create ExpressionProfile building block named arguments. thin factory around ExpressionProfile$new() builds raw list shape . ExpressionProfile's identity composite Molecule|Species|Category, three molecule, species, category required.

title: Create a new expression profile — create_expression_profile
code: create_expression_profile(   molecule,   species,   category,   type,   localization = NULL,   transport_type = NULL,   ontogeny = NULL,   parameters = NULL,   expression = NULL,   disease = NULL,   description = NULL )

title: Create a new expression profile — create_expression_profile
text: molecule Character. Name molecule (example "CYP3A4"). Required. species Character. Species (example "Human"). Required. category Character. Category disambiguator (example "Healthy" "Variability"). Required. type Character. Molecule type, typically one "Enzyme", "Transporter", "OtherProtein". Required. localization Character. Expression localization proteins. transport_type Character. Transporter type (transporter profiles), example "Efflux" "Influx". ontogeny Character list. Ontogeny name, raw ontogeny list. string supplied wrapped list(Name = ontogeny). parameters List Parameter objects (created create_parameter()) raw parameter lists describing relative expression per-organ values. expression Per-organ relative expression, written snapshot's Expression array. Supply data frame (tibble), one row per organ/compartment, required name column (organ/container name, example "Liver") optional columns value (relative expression), compartment (example "Intracellular"), transport_direction (transporter profiles). Missing (NA) absent optional cells emit key, row name value produces container just two fields. Row order preserved duplicate names kept. Alternatively supply raw list container lists (named list Name, Value, CompartmentName, TransportDirection) pass verbatim. NULL empty input writes nothing. disease Disease state, written snapshot's Disease object. Supply named list name (required, DiseaseState name) optional parameters list Parameter objects raw parameter lists. NULL writes nothing. description Character. Free-text description profile.

title: Create a new expression profile — create_expression_profile
Create a new expression profile — create_expression_profile
code: # Create a minimal enzyme expression profile profile <- create_expression_profile(   molecule = "CYP3A4",   species = "Human",   category = "Healthy",   type = "Enzyme" )  # Create a transporter expression profile with ontogeny profile <- create_expression_profile(   molecule = "P-gp",   species = "Human",   category = "Healthy",   type = "Transporter",   transport_type = "Efflux",   ontogeny = "P-gp" )  # Create an enzyme profile with per-organ relative expression profile <- create_expression_profile(   molecule = "CYP3A4",   species = "Human",   category = "Healthy",   type = "Enzyme",   expression = data.frame(     name = c("Liver", "Kidney"),     value = c(1, 0.5)   ) )

title: Create a new formulation — create_formulation
text: Create new formulation specified properties. arguments optional except name type.

title: Create a new formulation — create_formulation
Create a new formulation — create_formulation
text: name Character. Name formulation. type Character. Type formulation. curated human-readable alias known raw Formulation_* key resolves key: "Dissolved" - Immediate release solution "Weibull" - Weibull tablet formulation "Lint80" - Lint80 tablet formulation "Particle" - Particle formulation "Table" - Custom release profile table "Zero Order" - Zero-order release formulation "First Order" - First-order release formulation non-empty string accepted verbatim written straight FormulationType, new otherwise unknown PK-Sim template type can authored (mirroring create_event() treats template). curated parameter vocabulary exists known types. parameters List. Accepts either two mutually exclusive forms (never mixed one call): Curated form: named list drawn per-type alias vocabulary known type (documented sections ), scalar/vector values. Defaults, informational messages, alias--Name synthesis, built-Table shape apply. Invalid aliases error. Raw form: list Parameter objects (built create_parameter()) /raw list(Name=, Value=, ...) dicts. entry written data$Parameters Name, Value, Unit, ValueOrigin, TableFormula preserved verbatim, type. form unlocks arbitrary real parameter names (including Dissolved), per-parameter ValueOrigin, custom TableFormula type. raw form valid unknown type (alias vocabulary interpret curated-looking list ).

title: Create a new formulation — create_formulation
Create a new formulation — create_formulation
text: dissolution_time - Time achieve 50% dissolution (numeric, default: 240) dissolution_time_unit - Unit dissolution time (character, default: "min") lag_time - Lag time dissolution starts (numeric, default: 0) lag_time_unit - Unit lag time (character, default: "min") dissolution_shape - Dissolution shape parameter (numeric, default: 0.92) suspension - Whether use suspension (logical, default: TRUE)

title: Create a new formulation — create_formulation
text: dissolution_time - Time achieve 80% dissolution (numeric, default: 240) dissolution_time_unit - Unit dissolution time (character, default: "min") lag_time - Lag time dissolution starts (numeric, default: 0) lag_time_unit - Unit lag time (character, default: "min") suspension - Whether use suspension (logical, default: TRUE)

title: Create a new formulation — create_formulation
text: thickness - Thickness unstirred water layer (numeric, default: 30) thickness_unit - Unit thickness (character, default: "\u00b5m") distribution_type - Type distribution, "mono" "poly" (character, default: "mono") radius - Particle radius (mean geometric mean) (numeric, default: 10) radius_unit - Unit radius (character, default: "\u00b5m") Parameters polydisperse distribution (distribution_type = "poly"): particle_size_distribution - "normal" "lognormal" (character, default: "normal") radius_sd - Radius standard deviation, normal distribution (numeric, default: 3) radius_sd_unit - Unit radius SD (character, default: "\u00b5m") radius_cv - Coefficient variation, lognormal distribution (numeric, default: 1.5) radius_min - Minimum particle radius (numeric, default: 1) radius_min_unit - Unit minimum radius (character, default: "\u00b5m") radius_max - Maximum particle radius (numeric, default: 19) radius_max_unit - Unit maximum radius (character, default: "\u00b5m") n_bins - Number bins (integer, default: 3)

title: Create a new formulation — create_formulation
text: tableX - Vector time points release profile hours (numeric vector, required) tableY - Vector fraction dose time point (numeric vector, required) suspension - Whether use suspension (logical, default: TRUE)

title: Create a new formulation — create_formulation
text: end_time - Time administration end (numeric, default: 60) end_time_unit - Unit end time (character, default: "min")

title: Create a new formulation — create_formulation
text: thalf - Half-life drug release (numeric, default: 0.01) thalf_unit - Unit half-life (character, default: "min")

title: Create a new formulation — create_formulation
code: # Create a dissolved formulation (simplest type) dissolved <- create_formulation(name = "Oral Solution", type = "Dissolved")  # Create a Weibull tablet formulation tablet <- create_formulation(   name = "Standard Tablet",   type = "Weibull",   parameters = list(     dissolution_time = 60,     dissolution_time_unit = "min",     lag_time = 10,     lag_time_unit = "min",     dissolution_shape = 0.92,     suspension = TRUE   ) )  # Create a particle formulation with monodisperse distribution particle <- create_formulation(   name = "Suspension",   type = "Particle",   parameters = list(     thickness = 25,     thickness_unit = "µm",     radius = 5,     radius_unit = "µm"   ) ) #> No distribution_type provided, using default of mono  # Create a table formulation with custom release profile custom <- create_formulation(   name = "Custom Release",   type = "Table",   parameters = list(     tableX = c(0, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8),     tableY = c(0, 0.1, 0.3, 0.6, 0.9, 1.0),     suspension = TRUE   ) )  # Raw form: set an arbitrary parameter (with a ValueOrigin) on Dissolved raw_dissolved <- create_formulation(   name = "Suspension",   type = "Dissolved",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(       name = "Use as suspension",       value = 1,       source = "Lit",       description = "Reference XYZ"     )   ) )  # Raw form on an unknown type carrying a custom TableFormula custom_type <- create_formulation(   name = "Novel",   type = "Formulation_BrandNew",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(       name = "Fraction (dose)",       value = 0,       table_points = list(list(x = 0, y = 0), list(x = 60, y = 1)),       x_name = "Time",       y_name = "Fraction",       x_unit = "min",       x_dimension = "Time",       y_dimension = "Fraction"     )   ) )

code: # Create a minimal individual individual <- create_individual(name = "Test Individual")  # Create a complete individual individual <- create_individual(   name = "John Doe",   species = "Human",   population = "European_ICRP_2002",   gender = "MALE",   age = age(30),   weight = weight(70),   height = height(175) )  # Create an individual with calculation methods individual <- create_individual(   name = "Test Individual",   calculation_methods = c("Method 1", "Method 2", "Method 3") )  # Create an individual with disease state (emitted as the modern # `Disease` object: `OriginData$Disease = { Name, Parameters }`) individual <- create_individual(   name = "Patient",   disease_state = "CKD",   disease_state_parameters = list(     list(Name = "eGFR", Value = 45.0, Unit = "ml/min/1.73m²")   ) )  # Create an individual referencing expression profiles with a localized # parameter and a description individual <- create_individual(   name = "Subject 1",   expression_profiles = c("CYP3A4|Human|Healthy", "P-gp|Human|Healthy"),   description = "Reference healthy adult",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(       path = "Organism|Liver|EHC continuous fraction",       value = 1     )   ) )

title: Create a new observer — create_observer
text: name Character. Name observer (required). type Character. observer type, one "Amount" (AmountObserverBuilder) "Container" (ContainerObserverBuilder). value aborts (required). dimension Character. dimension observed quantity. Omitted NULL. formula observer's formula. Either bare expression string (becomes inner Formula$Formula), full ExplicitFormula raw list used whole Formula. Omitted NULL. formula_references List formula references, create_formula_reference() output equivalent raw {Alias, Path, Dimension} list. Written formula's References; supplying without formula yields Formula carrying References. formula full ExplicitFormula list already carries References, argument overrides . Omitted NULL. container_criteria List container criteria, create_descriptor_condition() output equivalent raw {Tag, Type} list. Written ContainerCriteria. Omitted NULL. molecule_list create_molecule_list() output equivalent raw list. Written MoleculeList. Omitted NULL.

title: Create a new observer — create_observer

code: # Minimal amount observer create_observer(name = "drug_amount", type = "Amount") #>  #> ── Observer: drug_amount  #> • Type: Amount  # Container observer with all sub-properties obs <- create_observer(   name = "brain_plasma_conc",   type = "Container",   dimension = "Concentration (molar)",   formula = "Conc_Br",   formula_references = list(     create_formula_reference(       "Conc_Br",       "Organism|Brain|Plasma|Drug|Concentration"     )   ),   container_criteria = list(     create_descriptor_condition("Brain", "InContainer")   ),   molecule_list = create_molecule_list(for_all = FALSE, include = "Drug") )  # Compose into an observer set create_observer_set(name = "BrainPlasmaConcentration", observers = list(obs)) #>  #> ── ObserverSet: BrainPlasmaConcentration ─────────────────────────────────────── #> • 1 observer

title: Create a new protocol — create_protocol
text: name Character. Name protocol (required). application_type Character. Application type Simple Protocol. Optional; supplied must one canonical PK-Sim application types: "Oral", "Intravenous", "IntravenousBolus", "UserDefined". Mutually exclusive schemas. dosing_interval Character. Dosing interval identifier Simple Protocol. Optional; supplied must one fixed PK-Sim DosingIntervalId values: "Single", "DI_6_6_6_6", "DI_6_6_12", "DI_8_8_8", "DI_12_12", "DI_24". target_organ Character. Target organ dose. valid application_type "UserDefined". target_compartment Character. Target compartment dose. valid application_type "UserDefined". parameters List Parameter objects (created create_parameter()) raw parameter lists. free-form escape hatch Simple-Protocol parameter dedicated argument. Dose, start time, end time dedicated dose, start_time, end_time arguments; supplying one settings via dedicated argument error. schemas List schemas Advanced Protocol. Entries may Schema objects (created create_schema()) raw schema lists Name, Parameters, SchemaItems. provided, protocol created Advanced Protocol. time_unit Character. Display time unit protocol, validated dimension "Time"; valid units ospsuite::ospUnits$Time. dose Numeric scalar. Optional dose Simple Protocol. supplied emitted single InputDose parameter carrying dose dose_unit. Mutually exclusive schemas, InputDose entry parameters. dose_unit Character. Display unit dose, default "mg". Validated dose-family unit: unit must belong one mass, amount, dose per body weight, dose per body surface area dimensions, unit alone selects family (emitted parameter always single InputDose). single ospsuite::ospUnits$Dose member, accepted units union ospsuite::ospUnits$Mass, ospsuite::ospUnits$Amount, ospsuite::ospUnits[["Dose per body weight"]], ospsuite::ospUnits[["Dose per body surface area"]]. consulted dose supplied. start_time Numeric scalar. Optional start time Simple Protocol. supplied emitted Start time parameter. Mutually exclusive schemas, Start time entry parameters. start_time_unit Character. Display unit start_time, default "h", validated dimension "Time"; valid units ospsuite::ospUnits$Time. consulted start_time supplied. end_time Numeric scalar. Optional end time Simple Protocol. supplied emitted End time parameter. display unit taken time_unit, falling back "h" time_unit unset; separate end-time unit argument. Mutually exclusive schemas, End time entry parameters.

title: Create a new protocol — create_protocol
Create a new protocol — create_protocol
code: # Create a simple oral protocol with one dose via promoted arguments protocol <- create_protocol(   name = "Single dose 10mg",   application_type = "Oral",   dosing_interval = "Single",   dose = 10,   dose_unit = "mg",   start_time = 0 )  # The same protocol via the free-form `parameters` escape hatch protocol <- create_protocol(   name = "Single dose 10mg",   application_type = "Oral",   dosing_interval = "Single",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(name = "Start time", value = 0, unit = "h"),     create_parameter(name = "InputDose", value = 10, unit = "mg")   ) )  # Create a twice-daily intravenous protocol protocol <- create_protocol(   name = "BID IV",   application_type = "IntravenousBolus",   dosing_interval = "DI_12_12",   parameters = list(     create_parameter(name = "InputDose", value = 5, unit = "mg")   ) )  # Create an Advanced Protocol from Schema objects protocol <- create_protocol(   name = "Advanced",   schemas = list(     create_schema(       name = "Schema 1",       parameters = list(         create_parameter(name = "NumberOfRepetitions", value = 1)       ),       items = list(         create_schema_item(           name = "Item 1",           application_type = "Oral",           parameters = list(             create_parameter(name = "InputDose", value = 5, unit = "mg")           )         )       )     )   ),   time_unit = "h" )

title: Create a new schema — create_schema
text: name Character. Name schema (required). parameters List Parameter objects (created create_parameter()) raw parameter lists. escape hatch schema-level parameter promoted plain argument. promoted argument mutually exclusive matching entry (entry named "NumberOfRepetitions", "TimeBetweenRepetitions", "Start time"): supply repetition parameter either plain argument parameters entry, . items List SchemaItem objects (created create_schema_item()) raw schema item lists. define applications inside schema. number_of_repetitions Numeric. Optional count schema repetitions (NumberOfRepetitions). single finite whole number (dimensionless, unit). Mutually exclusive "NumberOfRepetitions" entry parameters. time_between_repetitions Numeric. Optional time repetitions (TimeBetweenRepetitions). single finite numeric. Mutually exclusive "TimeBetweenRepetitions" entry parameters. time_between_repetitions_unit Character. Display unit time_between_repetitions, default "h", validated "Time" dimension. start_time Numeric. Optional schema start time (Start time). single finite numeric. Mutually exclusive "Start time" entry parameters. start_time_unit Character. Display unit start_time, default "h", validated "Time" dimension.

title: Create a new schema — create_schema

title: Create a new schema item — create_schema_item
text: name Character. Name schema item (required). application_type Character. Application type schema item (required). Must one canonical PK-Sim application types: "Oral", "Intravenous", "IntravenousBolus", "UserDefined". formulation_key Character. Formulation key linking schema item formulation selection owning simulation. target_organ Character. Target organ application. valid application_type "UserDefined". target_compartment Character. Target compartment application. valid application_type "UserDefined". parameters List Parameter objects (created create_parameter()) raw parameter lists. become application-level parameters (dose, start time, ...). promoted dose start_time arguments flow "InputDose" "Start time" parameters, supplying setting promoted argument matching parameters entry error. dose Numeric scalar dose application, written single InputDose parameter. NULL (default) emits dose parameter. dose family (plain dose, per body weight, per body surface area) selected dose_unit. dose_unit Character. Unit dose, default "mg". Solubility value object — solubility
text: Build solubility value object create_compound()'s solubility argument (writable Compound$solubility field). Expresses either scalar form (single solubility value reference pH, optionally solubility gain per charge) mutually exclusive table form (pH/value table). scalar-form arguments (value, reference_pH, gain_per_charge) table mutually exclusive: supplying table together error. Unlike previous create_compound() behaviour, reference_pH / gain_per_charge silently ignored table path, helper rejects combination intent unambiguous.

title: Solubility value object — solubility
code: solubility(   value = NULL,   unit = "mg/l",   reference_pH = NULL,   gain_per_charge = NULL,   table = NULL,   name = "User defined",   default = FALSE )

title: Solubility value object — solubility
text: value Numeric scalar. Solubility--reference-pH value scalar form. Leave NULL table form. unit Character. Unit solubility value (scalar form) table Y values, validated dimension "Concentration (mass)". Defaults "mg/l". reference_pH Numeric scalar. Reference pH added scalar Solubility alternative. Scalar form . gain_per_charge Numeric scalar. Optional Solubility gain per charge parameter added scalar Solubility alternative. Scalar form . table Two-column data frame giving table-based solubility: first column pH second solubility value. supplied, table form built scalar-form arguments must left unset. name Character. Name created alternative (scalar table). Defaults "User defined". default Logical. Whether alternative group's default appears list alternatives passed matching create_compound() argument Compound field. Defaults FALSE. Ignored single (non-list) value object, always default. list element marked default = TRUE, first element default (unchanged behaviour); marking two elements default = TRUE list error.
